links.targetTitle: The DocumentToolbar className prop merges with defaults via twMerge
Toolbar buttons must be styled via Tailwind arbitrary child selectors since they have no dedicated style props
Revision History panel styling requires scoped CSS on document-editor-context since no props are exposed

title: DocumentToolbar has three independent layers of styling control
content:
The `DocumentToolbar` component provides a layered styling architecture with clear separation of concerns:

## Layer 1: Container
The toolbar's outer container (background, border, rounding) is controlled via the `className` prop, which uses `twMerge` to merge custom classes with defaults. Default classes: `flex h-12 w-full items-center justify-between rounded-xl border border-gray-200 bg-slate-50 px-4`.

## Layer 2: Buttons
Internal buttons (undo, redo, export, history, close) use hardcoded Tailwind classes and don't expose their own style props. Customization requires Tailwind arbitrary child selectors passed through the parent `className` prop.

## Layer 3: Revision History Panel
The revision history panel is rendered by Connect outside the editor component tree. It lives inside `#document-editor-context` in the DOM and can only be styled via scoped CSS in `style.css`.

This three-layer architecture means theming a toolbar requires understanding which technique applies at each level — direct className for the container, child selectors for buttons, and external CSS for the history panel.
description: DocumentToolbar styling has three layers: container via className with twMerge, buttons via CSS child selectors, and Revision History via scoped CSS.
